blurb:
This is hands down the best Middle Eastern food in East Cobb, and the prices make it perfect for a casual family dinner or quick lunch. It's in a strip center but don't let that fool you. The inside is clean and welcoming, and the staff treats you like family.

Their shawarma is what keeps me coming back, and the falafel is crispy outside and perfectly seasoned inside. The portions are huge for the price, so one platter easily feeds two people if you're not super hungry. They also have great vegetarian options if that's your thing.

Open every day until 9pm, so it works for those weeknight dinners when you want something different but don't want to spend a fortune. The takeout is fast too if you're grabbing dinner on the way home.
address: 1175 Franklin Gateway SE, Marietta, GA 30067, USA

blurb:
This is perfect when your group can't agree on what to eat because there are twenty different restaurants all in one place. It's right on the square so you can walk around downtown Marietta before or after eating. Great for families with picky kids or when you're meeting friends who all want different things.

The variety is impressive and ranges from casual to more upscale options. I've tried several of the vendors and the quality is consistently good across the board. It's also nice for browsing the retail shops while you're there.

Parking can be tricky during busy weekends since you're right downtown, but it's worth the short walk. The atmosphere is lively and it stays open later on weekends if you want dinner and then drinks on the square.
address: 68 North Marietta Pkwy NW, Marietta, GA 30060, USA
hours: Mon-Thu 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM, Fri-Sat 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Sun 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM

blurb: This is where we go for special occasions when we want to feel fancy without driving downtown. It sits right on the Chattahoochee River, and if you get a window table, the view is really something. Perfect for anniversaries, milestone birthdays, or impressing out-of-town visitors. The seafood is what they do best, and the Southern touches make everything feel familiar but elevated. My wife always gets something with shrimp, and I usually go for whatever fish special they're running. Just know you're paying for the experience and location, but it's worth it when you want to celebrate something important.
address: 6700 Powers Ferry Rd NW, Sandy Springs, GA 30339, USA

blurb:
If you've never done Brazilian all-you-can-eat, this is the place to try it without feeling intimidated. The atmosphere is relaxed and the staff does a great job explaining how everything works. Perfect for celebrating something special or when you want to try a bunch of different things.

The barbecue keeps coming to your table and the cheese bread alone is worth the trip. They also have feijoada stew on certain days which is their traditional comfort food. Don't skip the salad bar either because it has way more than just salad.

Come hungry because you'll definitely get your money's worth. They serve breakfast too, which is something different for weekend mornings when you want to mix things up.
address: 1275 Powers Ferry Rd ste 230, Marietta, GA 30067, USA
hours: Mon-Thu 10:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Fri-Sat 10:00 AM – 11:00 PM, Sun 10:00 AM – 10:00 PM

blurb: The hype is real here, and that's saying something in a town full of taco places. It's in Sandy Springs Place with a vibrant atmosphere that makes you want to stay and hang out. Perfect for girls' night out, post-game dinners, or any time you want tacos that actually taste authentic. Start with the Esquite because it's packed with flavor and gets the table going. The cocktail pitchers are dangerous in the best way. The Pineapple Guava and Passion Fruit ones are perfectly balanced and strong enough to share. The service is attentive without being pushy, and they're open until midnight most nights, which is clutch when you need late-night food that doesn't suck.
address: 227 Sandy Springs Pl NE #506, Sandy Springs, GA 30328, USA

blurb:
This is as close to France as you'll get in Marietta, and the 4.9 star rating tells you everything you need to know. It's perfect for a special weekend brunch or when you want to grab really good pastries and coffee. The atmosphere is bright and welcoming, and you can tell everything is made fresh daily.

Their baguettes are the real deal, and the quiches make a great light lunch. The petits fours are beautiful if you need something for a special occasion. I also love coming here for a quiet morning with coffee and a croissant when I want to feel fancy.

Note that Sunday hours are shorter and they close at 3:30pm, so plan accordingly for weekend visits. It's worth the trip even if you're coming from farther out in East Cobb.
address: 277 South Marietta Pkwy SW, Marietta, GA 30064, USA
hours: Mon-Sat 7:00 AM – 6:00 PM, Sun 9:00 AM – 3:30 PM

blurb:
This is the kind of place that saves you when nothing else is open and you need real food fast. It's been a Marietta landmark forever with that classic neon sign, and being open 24/7 means it's perfect for late night cravings or early morning meetings. The atmosphere is pure old-school diner with booths and that familiar buzz of conversation.

The menu is massive and they do both American classics and Greek specialties really well. I always get their big breakfast platters when I'm there early, but the Greek dishes are what set them apart from other diners. The portions are generous and the coffee keeps coming.

Just know you might wait a bit during peak weekend brunch hours, but the turnover is pretty quick. It's cash and card friendly, and there's plenty of parking which is nice for Cobb Parkway.
address: 306 Cobb Pkwy SE, Marietta, GA 30060, USA

blurb: This is hands down one of my top dinner spots in Atlanta. It's in the Aria development in Sandy Springs, and the whole experience feels special from the moment you walk in. Perfect for date night, girls' night out, or any time you want to try something different that'll blow you away. Start with the hummus and wings, which sounds weird but trust me on this combination. The chicken shish kabobs with rice, lentils, and raisins are what I always end up ordering. The cocktails are solid, and you have to save room for the tiramisu because it's the perfect way to end the meal. Just know they break between lunch and dinner, so plan accordingly. This place is worth the drive from anywhere in the area.
address: 6500 Aria Blvd Suite 500, Sandy Springs, GA 30328, USA

blurb: This is our go-to spot when we want real Indian food without the fuss. It's in a Perimeter Center strip mall, and the inside is colorful but not fancy. Perfect for families or when you want good comfort food that won't break the bank. The biryani is what they're known for, and it comes out family-style in big portions that are great for sharing. My kids love the chicken tikka masala, and they're good about adjusting spice levels if you ask. The service is friendly and they don't rush you, which I appreciate when we're there with the whole family.
address: 130 Perimeter Center Pl, Dunwoody, GA 30346, USA

restaurant_name: Mister O1 Extraordinary Pizza Sandy Springs
blurb: This place has ruined me for regular pizza. It's tucked into a shopping center on Blue Stone Road, but don't let that fool you. The thin crust is what dreams are made of, and they use ingredients imported from Italy or sourced locally. Great for date night or when friends are coming over and you want to order something that'll actually impress them. The Margherita is perfect if you want to taste what real pizza should be like. Their salads and antipasti are also solid if you want to make it a full meal. Just be prepared to wait a little longer than typical pizza places because they're making everything from scratch.
address: 6405 Blue Stone Rd Ste 240, Atlanta, GA 30328, USA
